questionText: Which sentence correctly expresses a Type 2 conditional (hypothetical present) ?
options: If it rains, we will cancel the match.
If it rained, we would cancel the match.
If it had rained, we would have cancelled the match.
If it rains, we cancel the match.
explanation: Type 2 conditionnel exprime une hypothèse irréelle au présent ou au futur. La structure est : If + past simple, would + base verb. 'If it rained, we would cancel the match' signifie qu'il ne pleut pas actuellement, mais si c'était le cas, on annulerait le match.

questionText: How do you form the Type 3 conditional (unreal past) ?
options: If + present simple → will + verb
If + past simple → would + verb
If + past perfect → would have + past participle
If + present perfect → would + have + past participle
explanation: Le Type 3 conditionnel sert à exprimer un regret ou une situation imaginaire dans le passé. La structure correcte est : If + past perfect (had + participe passé), would have + participe passé. Exemple : If I had studied, I would have passed.

questionText: Complete the sentence: "If I ______ you, I ______ that job." (Type 2)
options: was / will accept
were / would accept
had been / would have accepted
am / accept
explanation: Avec le verbe 'to be' au Type 2, on utilise 'were' pour toutes les personnes (I, he, she, it). C'est l'irréel du présent. La structure est : If + I/he/she/it + were, would + base verb. 'If I were you, I would accept' (Si j'étais toi, j'accepterais).

questionText: Choose the correct sentence to express regret about the past (Type 3):
options: If she studies, she will succeed.
If she studied, she would succeed.
If she had studied, she would succeed.
If she had studied, she would have succeeded.
explanation: Pour exprimer un regret (quelque chose qui ne s'est pas produit dans le passé), on utilise le Type 3. La phrase signifie : 'Si elle avait étudié (mais elle ne l'a pas fait), elle aurait réussi (mais elle a échoué).' Structure : If + past perfect, would have + past participle.

questionText: "He didn't wake up early, so he missed his flight." Transform this sentence into a Type 3 conditional.
options: If he wakes up early, he will not miss his flight.
If he woke up early, he would not miss his flight.
If he had woken up early, he would not have missed his flight.
If he had woke up early, he would not miss his flight.
explanation: Il faut inverser la réalité passée. Réalité : Il ne s'est pas réveillé tôt → Il a raté son vol. Hypothèse contraire : S'il s'était réveillé tôt (had woken up), il n'aurait pas raté son vol (would not have missed). Attention : 'woken' est le participe passé de 'wake'.

questionText: Which sentence correctly mixes a Type 3 condition (past) with a Type 2 consequence (present) ?
options: If I had taken the train, I would be in Paris now.
If I took the train, I would be in Paris now.
If I had taken the train, I would have been in Paris now.
If I take the train, I will be in Paris now.
explanation: C'est un conditionnel mixte. La condition est dans le passé (Type 3 : If I had taken the train) mais le résultat est dans le présent (Type 2 : I would be in Paris now). On l'utilise quand une action passée non réalisée affecte la situation présente.

questionText: Fill in the blank: "They missed the bus. If they ______ (run) faster, they ______ (catch) it." (Type 3)
options: ran / would catch
had run / would have caught
have run / will catch
were running / would catch
explanation: La situation est au passé (ils ont raté le bus). Pour exprimer une hypothèse contraire à la réalité passée, on utilise le Type 3 : If + past perfect (had run) + would have + past participle (would have caught).

questionText: What is the main difference between Type 2 and Type 3 conditionals?
options: Type 2 uses 'will', Type 3 uses 'would'.
Type 2 is for the future, Type 3 is for the present.
Type 2 is for unreal present/future, Type 3 is for unreal past.
Type 2 uses 'had', Type 3 uses 'was'.
explanation: Le Type 2 imagine une situation différente dans le présent ou le futur (If I were rich...). Le Type 3 imagine une situation différente dans le passé, souvent pour exprimer des regrets (If I had known...).

questionText: Identify the correct inverted form (without 'if') of a Type 3 conditional: "If she had known, she would have helped."
options: Had she known, she would have helped.
She had known, would she have helped.
Did she know, she would have helped.
Has she known, she would have helped.
explanation: Dans un style formel ou littéraire, on peut supprimer 'if' et inverser le sujet et l'auxiliaire 'had' pour les conditionnels de Type 3. Had she known = If she had known. C'est une compétence exigée par le programme (Master conditional inversions - Lesson 11, mais souvent liée aux Types 2 et 3).

questionText: Choose the correct option: "If we ______ (be) in 1985, we ______ (use) a payphone instead of a smartphone." (Type 2)
options: are / will use
were / would use
had been / would have used
have been / use
explanation: Nous ne sommes pas en 1985 (réalité présente différente). C'est une hypothèse sur le présent. Structure Type 2 : If + past simple ('were' pour toutes les personnes) + would + base verb ('would use').
